name: Serra de Galliners Loop
name_original: Terrassa, Serra de Galliners, Camí del Mig – Ruta Circular
description: This moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) jogging route in Serra de Galliners offers varied terrain and panoramic views from Pujol Blanc.
region_intro: Jogging around Terrassa offers diverse landscapes, from urban green spaces to natural park areas. The region features varied terrain, including the rolling hills of Serra de Galliners and the more challenging ascents around Turó de l'Ós and Turó de Sant Joan. Extensive parks like Vallparadís Park provide accessible paths, while areas closer to Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park offer more rugged trails. Best jogging routes around Terrassa The most popular jogging route is Serra de Galliners Loop, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain and panoramic views from Pujol Blanc, with mostly paved trails suitable for different skill levels. Another top favourite among local runners is Castell de Les Fonts – Monks' Path loop from Les Fonts, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This longer route features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced runners seeking a challenging workout. Local runners also love the Trail to the Lake – Turó de Sant Joan loop from Terrassa Nacions Unides, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through challenging terrain with notable elevation changes, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Terrassa is defined by varied terrain, urban green spaces, and natural park areas, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Terrassa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 37 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Terrassa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Terrassa?
A: Terrassa offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 160 options available. These range from easy paths suitable for beginners to challenging trails for experienced runners, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Terrassa?
A: Yes, Terrassa has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963960973/serra-de-galliners-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Galliners Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) trail with mostly paved sections, offering varied terrain and panoramic views from Pujol Blanc, making it accessible for different skill levels. Additionally, urban green spaces like Vallparadís Park provide extensive, flat trails ideal for leisurely jogs.

Q: What are some challenging trails for experienced runners in Terrassa?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Terrassa offers several demanding trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10497522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Les Fonts – Monks' Path loop from Les Fonts&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile (19.0 km) route with significant elevation gain. Other tough options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19192553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Ós – The Diagonal Trail loop from Terrassa Nacions Unides&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14995815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Candi loop from Terrassa Estació del Nord&lt;/a&gt;, both categorized as hard runs with considerable elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Terrassa area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes around Terrassa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963960973/serra-de-galliners-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Galliners Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Lake – Turó de Sant Joan loop from Terrassa Nacions Unides&lt;/a&gt;, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10497522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Les Fonts – Monks' Path loop from Les Fonts&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Terrassa suitable for families or running with kids?
A: Yes, Terrassa has options suitable for families and running with kids, particularly in its urban green spaces. Vallparadís Park, for example, offers extensive, flat trails, picnic areas, and play areas, making it ideal for a family outing. For slightly older children, some of the easier, paved sections of routes like the Serra de Galliners Loop could also be suitable.

Q: What kind of natural features or attractions can I see while jogging around Terrassa?
A: Jogging around Terrassa offers diverse scenery. You can enjoy panoramic views from Pujol Blanc on the Serra de Galliners Loop. Within the city, Vallparadís Park features a scenic stream and lush greenery. For those exploring further, the region is close to natural parks with highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3779551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Dancing Holm Oak&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3502692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Estenalles&lt;/a&gt;. The area also boasts several gorges, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2956234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Escletxes del Papiol&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg d'en Fitó&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How accessible are the running routes in Terrassa by public transport?
A: Many running routes in Terrassa are accessible by public transport, especially those starting from within the city or near train stations. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Lake – Turó de Sant Joan loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19192553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Ós – The Diagonal Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; both start from Terrassa Nacions Unides, which is a train station. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14995815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Candi loop&lt;/a&gt; also begins near Terrassa Estació del Nord. It's advisable to check local bus and train schedules for specific starting points.

Q: Are there specific parking facilities near popular running trails?
A: Yes, many popular running trails in and around Terrassa offer convenient parking. For urban parks like Vallparadís Park, there are often nearby street parking options or dedicated parking lots. For routes that venture into natural areas, trailheads typically have designated parking spaces. It's always a good idea to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information before you go.

Q: What is the best season for running in Terrassa?
A: Terrassa offers good running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter running is also feasible, though some higher elevation trails might require appropriate gear for cooler temperatures.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Terrassa?
A: The running experience in Terrassa is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 37 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, from urban green spaces to natural park areas, and appreciate the options available for different ability levels. The scenic views and well-maintained paths are also commonly highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options near Terrassa?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, Terrassa's proximity to natural parks like Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac provides ample opportunities. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10497522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Les Fonts – Monks' Path loop from Les Fonts&lt;/a&gt;, at 11.8 miles (19.0 km), offer a significant distance challenge. The interconnected trail networks in these natural areas allow for extended runs, often with considerable elevation changes.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the running routes?
A: Yes, you can find cafes and restaurants near many of Terrassa's running routes, especially those within or close to urban areas. Vallparadís Park, being a central urban oasis, often has cafes nearby. For routes venturing into the natural parks, you might find establishments in nearby villages or at specific points of interest. It's a good idea to plan your route and check for amenities along the way.
tips: Can Grau and Can Falguera fountain (fountain of the five injectors). This fountain is reconstructed with stream stone and has five injectors. It is one of the elements that gave the sector its name and one of the most famous. It was located under the Can Falguera farmhouse, next to the En Grau Kiosk. The Grau family was one of the first to settle in Les Fonts and helped with the development.

The section of the Camí dels Monjos from Can Barnola to Pujol Blanc. It offers good views on both sides.

The Camí dels Monjos is a historical path that connects the old monasteries of Sant Cugat del Vallès and Sant Llorenç del Munt. It is currently signposted as a small PR-C 31 path

The legend says that the abbot and the monks of Sant Llorenç del Munt requested permission to the bishop of Barcelona to settle in another place, since their lands were not productive at all and the isolation of the monastery made them suffer evil of sadness The bishop allowed them to leave whenever they respected a mandate that walking without crossing any river, torrent or stream, would settle where better it was and this was what they used. For this reason, the path follows the ridge that separates the basin of the Besòs (east) of the Llobregat (west), or those of its tributaries, the Ripoll river and the Rubí stream, without crossing any course of water

Path in the middle of the wood that is not very busy and very pleasant, although quite demanding for its unevenness.

Due to the type of terrain, it is not recommended after heavy rains because it is very muddy and sticky.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered a moderate run, requiring a good level of fitness. The terrain is varied, with much of it consisting of mostly paved surfaces, but also includes accessible paths that may require some sure-footedness. You'll jog through Mediterranean forests, agricultural fields, and alongside torrents.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 10.4 kilometers (6.5 miles) long. For jogging, it typically takes around 1 hour and 10 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the Can Grau and Can Falguera Spring. The trail also incorporates sections of the historic Monks' Path (Can Barnola–El Pujol Blanc). From higher points like the Pujol Blanc, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Sant Llorenç and Montseny mountains, and even the Collserola mountain range and Montserrat in the distance, weather permitting.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it merges with parts of the historic Camí dels Monjos and the Camí del Mig. Other official trails you might encounter include Circular d'Ègara - Terrassa, Sender de Montserrat, and Senders del 1714 - Etapa 02.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs are not detailed in the provided information. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural fields or near wildlife, and to always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer mornings or late afternoons can also be suitable to avoid the midday heat. Visibility for panoramic views from Pujol Blanc can be affected by fog, so check local weather forecasts.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Serra de Galliners area?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Galliners is a natural space, but there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing the trails within this area. It's always good practice to respect local regulations and signage regarding conservation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking locations are not detailed, but as a circular route starting and ending in Terrassa, you would typically look for parking options within or on the outskirts of Terrassa that provide convenient access to the route's starting point. Public transport options in Terrassa could also lead you close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful scenery. The route takes you through extensive Mediterranean forests with holm oaks, pine trees, and oaks, as well as agricultural fields. You'll also get views of the plains of Vallès, and from higher elevations, distant mountain ranges like Sant Llorenç, Montseny, Collserola, and Montserrat.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate jogging footwear, comfortable athletic clothing, and enough water to stay hydrated. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is recommended, especially during sunnier months. A small backpack for essentials and a phone for navigation or emergencies would also be useful.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Terrassa, so you'll find various facilities like cafes, restaurants, and restrooms within the town itself. Along the natural sections of the trail, facilities are limited, so it's best to plan accordingly before you start your run.
